art_market.summary: The Korean art market is undergoing structural transformation, shifting towards greater diversity and accessibility with increased participation from Millennial and Gen Z collectors. Mid-to-lower priced segments are currently more active than high-end transactions.
key_events: 20!2026 Art Seoul: Digital exhibit focused on trust and accessibility with an '80% refund guarantee' system.
Major Exhibitions: Perrotin's '10 Years' (until May 2) and the upcoming MMCA Damien Hirst retrospective.
Upcoming: Galleries Art Fair (April 8-12) at COEX with record 169 galleries; Korean Cultural Center exhibition opening March 24.
art_market.outlook: Cautiously optimistic; focus on structural adaptation rather than immediate economic rebound.

stock_market.summary: The KOSPI closed at 5,781.2 on March 20, 2026. Despite extreme volatility earlier in March due to geopolitical risks, the market shows underlying strength supported by semiconductor earnings and high corporate profit expectations for 2026.

key_indicators.KOSPI_Status: Rallying from recent volatility; navigating geopolitical sensitivities.
key_indicators.Market_Sentiment: Cautiously optimistic; concerns over overheating balanced by strong fundamentals.
key_indicators.Key_Drivers: Semiconductor supercycle, strong corporate earnings (shipbuilding, defense), and domestic retail buying.
key_indicators.Outlook: Potential for further upside; major brokerage targets (Goldman Sachs 7,000 / Morgan Stanley 6,500) suggest room for growth if fundamentals hold.
